lede: A look at February 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including I.N and Carolina Kostner, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Laval daycare bus crash
events.desc: Two children are killed and six others are injured when a bus crashes into a daycare centre in Laval, Quebec, Canada. The driver is arrested and charged with homicide and dangerous driving.

events.title: Iran Airtour
events.desc: An Iran Air Tours Tupolev Tu-154 and an Iranian Air Force Sukhoi Su-24 collide mid-air near Qods, Iran, killing all 133 people on board both aircraft.

events.title: Army of the Republic of Vietnam
events.desc: South Vietnamese ground troops launch an incursion into Laos to try to cut off the Ho Chi Minh trail and stop communist infiltration into the country.

events.title: Stasi
events.desc: The Stasi, the secret police of East Germany, is established.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: Republican forces establish the Interprovincial Council of Santander, Palencia and Burgos in Cantabria.

events.title: Japanese in Hawaii
events.desc: The first Japanese immigrants arrive in Hawaii.

events.title: Richard Mentor Johnson
events.desc: Richard Johnson becomes the first and only Vice President of the United States chosen by the Senate.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon defeats the coalition forces of Russian General Bennigsen and Prussian General L'Estocq at the Battle of Eylau.

events.title: Mary, Queen of Scots
events.desc: Mary, Queen of Scots is executed on suspicion of having been involved in the Babington Plot to murder her cousin, Queen Elizabeth I.

events.title: Seventh Crusade
events.desc: Seventh Crusade: Crusaders engage Ayyubid forces in the Battle of Al Mansurah.

births.name: Nicolas Vouilloz
births.line: French rally driver and mountain biker

births.name: Santosh Sivan
births.line: Indian director, cinematographer, producer, and actor

births.name: Ba Maw
births.line: Burmese lawyer and politician, Prime Minister of Burma (died 1977)

deaths.text: Dick Jauron, American football player and coach
William Lyons, English businessman, co-founded Swallow Sidecar Company
